label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage carefully to figure out the correct answer. The passage discusses how memory can play tricks on us, providing examples of false memories. In the first example, the writer says: "One morning last week, for example, I got up and found that I had left the front door unlocked all night, yet I clearly remember locking it carefully the night before." This means that the writer found the door unlocked in the morning, but remembers locking it the previous night. So the writer believes they locked the door (this is their memory), but in reality, the door was left unlocked. Let's look at the options: (A) One night the writer forgot to lock the front door. - This is what actually happened according to the passage. The writer found the door unlocked in the morning, so they must have forgotten to lock it the previous night. (B) One night the writer forgot having locked the front door. - This suggests that the writer actually locked the door but then forgot that they did so. However, the passage says that the writer "clearly remember locking it carefully the night before," so they didn't forget having locked it. (C) The writer remembered to lock the door. - This is what the writer's memory tells them. The passage says they "clearly remember locking it carefully the night before," so they remembered locking it in their mind. (D) the writer remembered unlocking the front door. -
